title: Centrifugal compressor with labyrinth seal
abstract: A compressor for an HVAC system includes a stator and a rotor. The stator has a stator profile. The rotor has a rotor profile. The rotor is rotatable about a rotational axis. The stator and the rotor are separated from each other by a gap formed between the stator profile and the rotor profile. The gap includes an inlet, an outlet, and at least two cavities connected by a channel. Each cavity includes a first concave portion defined by the stator profile and a second concave portion defined by the rotor profile. The first concave portion is positioned at least partially opposite from the second concave portion. The first concave portion extends in a first direction and the second concave portion extends in a second direction opposite to the first direction. The first direction and the second direction are within about 45 degrees of opposite of each other.
claims: Claims ( 22 ) A compressor for an HVAC system comprising: a stator having a stator profile; and a rotor having a rotor profile, the rotor being rotatable about a rotational axis; the stator and the rotor being separated from each other by a gap formed between the stator profile and the rotor profile; the gap including an inlet, an outlet, and at least two cavities connected by a channel, each cavity including a first concave portion defined by the stator profile and a second concave portion defined by the rotor profile, the first concave portion being positioned at least partially opposite from the second concave portion, the first concave portion extending in a first direction and the second concave portion extending in a second direction opposite to the first direction, the first direction and the second direction being within about 45 degrees of opposite of each other. The compressor according to claim 1, wherein the portion of a channel defined by the stator profile is connected to a portion of a cavity defined by the stator profile. The compressor according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the portion of a channel defined by the rotor profile is connected to a portion of a cavity defined by the rotor profile. The compressor according to any of of claims 1 to 3, wherein the first direction and the second direction are between approximately 40 degrees and approximately 90 degrees from the rotational axis. The compressor according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the gap includes at least three cavities and a plurality of channels, each channel of the plurality of channels connecting two cavities adjacently disposed in a flow direction through the gap. The compressor according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the gap has an average direction, the average direction of the gap being defined by a line connecting the inlet to the outlet. The compressor according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein each channel has a first end and a second end, each cavity has an inlet end and an outlet end, a first distance between the inlet end and the outlet end of each cavity being at least 1/5 of a second distance between the first end and the second end of each channel. The compressor according to claim 4, wherein a first channel extends between a first cavity and a second cavity, and a second channel extends between the second cavity and a third cavity, the first and second channels being non-collinear. A chiller system comprising: a condenser; an evaporator; a compressor including an impeller assembly; an input suction line for gas refrigerant movement from the evaporator to the compressor; an output discharge line for gas refrigerant movement from the compressor to the condenser; and a motor assembly configured to drive the compressor; the impeller assembly including a stator having a stator profile; and a rotor having a rotor profile, the rotor being rotatable about a rotational axis, the stator and the rotor being separated from each other by a gap formed between the stator profile and the rotor profile; the gap including an inlet, an outlet, and at least two cavities connected by a channel, each cavity including a first concave portion defined by the stator profile and a second concave portion defined by the rotor profile, the first concave portion being positioned at least partially opposite from the second concave portion. The chiller system according to claim 9, wherein the gap includes at least three cavities and a plurality of channels, each channel of the plurality of channels connecting two cavities adjacently disposed in a flow direction through the gap. The chiller system according to claim 10, wherein a first channel extends between a first cavity and a second cavity, and a second channel extends between the second cavity and a third cavity, the first and second channels being non-collinear. The chiller system according to any one of claims 9 to 11, wherein each channel has a first end and a second end, each cavity has an inlet end and an outlet end, a first distance between the inlet end and the outlet end of each cavity being at least 1/5 of a second distance between the first end and the second end of each channel. The chiller system according to any one of claims 9 to 12, wherein the gap has an average direction, the average direction of the gap being defined by a line connecting the inlet to the outlet. The chiller system according to any one of claims 9 to 13, wherein the first concave portion extends in a first direction and the second concave portion extends in a second direction, the first direction and the second direction being between approximately forty degrees and approximately 90 degrees from the rotational axis. The chiller system according to any one of claims 9 to 14, wherein the refrigerant is a low global warming potential refrigerant. The chiller system according to claim 15, wherein the refrigerant is a low pressure refrigerant. A stepped labyrinth seal for rotatable members in a compressor assembly comprising: a stator having a stator profile; and a rotor having a rotor profile, the rotor being rotatable about a rotational axis; the stator and the rotor being separated from each other by a gap formed between the stator profile and the rotor profile; the gap including an inlet, an outlet, and at least two cavities connected by a channel, each cavity including a first concave portion defined by the stator profile and a second concave portion being defined by the rotor profile, the first concave portion being positioned at least partially opposite from the second concave portion; and the channel including a plurality of grooves. The compressor assembly according to claim 17, wherein each channel has a first end and a second end, each cavity has an inlet end and an outlet end, a first distance between the inlet end and the outlet end of each cavity being at least 1/5 of a second distance between the first end and the second end of each channel. The compressor assembly according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the gap includes at least three cavities and a plurality of channels, each channel of the plurality of channels connecting two cavities adjacently disposed in a flow direction through the gap. The compressor assembly according to claim 19, wherein a first channel extends between a first cavity and a second cavity, and a second channel extends between the second cavity and a third cavity, the first and second channels being non-collinear. The compressor assembly according to claim 20, wherein the first channel has a first plurality of grooves, and the second channel has a second plurality of grooves. The compressor assembly according to any one of claims 17 to 21, wherein the gap has an average direction, the average direction of the gap being defined by a line connecting the inlet to the outlet.
